message:
fix(websocket): harden frame codec for RFC 6455 compliance — minimal encoding, MSB, close reason

Frame::close: When called with a reason but no status code (None, Some(r)),
previously silently dropped the reason text. Per RFC 6455 §5.5.1, if a
Close frame body is present it MUST begin with a 2-byte status code. Now
defaults to Normal Close (1000) and includes the reason, matching the spec
and preventing silent data loss.

Decoder: Add three new validation checks in the extended-length parsing
path:
- Non-minimal 2-byte encoding rejected: values < 126 encoded in the
  2-byte extended form are a protocol violation (should use 7-bit form)
- Non-minimal 8-byte encoding rejected: values < 65536 encoded in the
  8-byte extended form are a protocol violation (should use 2-byte form)
- MSB validation: the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload length
  MUST be 0 per RFC 6455 §5.2

All three return WsError::ProtocolViolation and reset the state machine
to DecodeState::Header so the codec remains usable for subsequent frames.

Adds comprehensive test coverage:
- Reserved bits (RSV1) rejection
- Unmasked client frame rejection (server codec)
- Fragmented control frame rejection
- Control frame extended length rejection
- Multiple frames in single buffer (state machine reset)
- Close frame with reason but no code defaults to 1000
- Non-minimal 2-byte and 8-byte length rejection
- 64-bit MSB-set rejection
- Valid 2-byte length (126) acceptance (positive test)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
